problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums representing a singly linked list (head first), return a list representing the same nodes in reverse order.
problems.reference_solution:
def reverse_linked_list(nums):
    return list(nums)[::-1]

variants.name: returns-input
variants.code:
def reverse_linked_list(nums):
    return list(nums)

variants.name: drops-head
variants.code:
def reverse_linked_list(nums):
    return list(nums)[1:][::-1]

variants.name: sorts-instead
variants.code:
def reverse_linked_list(nums):
    return sorted(nums, reverse=True)

problems.title: Palindrome Linked List
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums representing a singly linked list, return True iff the values read the same forwards and backwards.
problems.reference_solution:
def is_palindrome_list(nums):
    return list(nums) == list(nums)[::-1]

variants.name: returns-true
variants.code:
def is_palindrome_list(nums):
    return True

variants.name: returns-false
variants.code:
def is_palindrome_list(nums):
    return False

variants.name: compares-with-sorted
variants.code:
def is_palindrome_list(nums):
    return list(nums) == sorted(nums)

problems.title: Middle of Linked List
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums representing a singly linked list, return the middle value. For even-length lists, return the second middle (e.g. for [1,2,3,4] return 3).
problems.reference_solution:
def middle_value(nums):
    return nums[len(nums) // 2]

variants.name: returns-first
variants.code:
def middle_value(nums):
    return nums[0]

variants.name: off-by-one-middle
variants.code:
def middle_value(nums):
    return nums[(len(nums) - 1) // 2]

variants.name: returns-last
variants.code:
def middle_value(nums):
    return nums[-1]

problems.title: Find Kth Largest
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums and an integer k (1-indexed), return the k-th largest element in nums. The list may contain duplicates and k is guaranteed to be valid (1 <= k <= len(nums)).
problems.reference_solution:
def find_kth_largest(nums, k):
    return sorted(nums, reverse=True)[k - 1]

variants.name: returns-max
variants.code:
def find_kth_largest(nums, k):
    return max(nums)

variants.name: zero-indexed
variants.code:
def find_kth_largest(nums, k):
    return sorted(nums, reverse=True)[k]

variants.name: kth-smallest
variants.code:
def find_kth_largest(nums, k):
    return sorted(nums)[k - 1]

problems.title: Remove Element by Value
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums and a value v, return a new list with every occurrence of v removed, preserving the order of remaining elements.
problems.reference_solution:
def remove_element(nums, v):
    return [n for n in nums if n != v]

variants.name: returns-input

    return list(nums)

variants.name: removes-first-only
inverted-condition
variants.code:

    return [n for n in nums if n == v]

problems.title: Intersection of Two Arrays
problems.problem_text: Given two lists of integers a and b, return a sorted list of distinct values that appear in both. The result must be sorted ascending and contain no duplicates.
problems.reference_solution:
def intersect_arrays(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) & set(b))

variants.name: returns-empty
variants.code:
def intersect_arrays(a, b):
    return []

variants.name: union-instead
variants.code:
def intersect_arrays(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) | set(b))

variants.name: no-dedup
variants.code:
def intersect_arrays(a, b):
    return sorted(x for x in a if x in b)

problems.title: Symmetric Difference
problems.problem_text: Given two lists of integers a and b, return a sorted list of distinct values that appear in exactly one of the two lists (in a XOR b). Result is sorted ascending with no duplicates.
problems.reference_solution:
def sym_diff(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) ^ set(b))

variants.name: intersection-not-xor
variants.code:
def sym_diff(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) & set(b))

variants.name: union-not-xor
variants.code:
def sym_diff(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) | set(b))

variants.name: diff-one-way
variants.code:
def sym_diff(a, b):
    return sorted(set(a) - set(b))

problems.title: Flatten Nested List (1-level)
problems.problem_text: Given a list of lists nested, return a flat list containing all elements in left-to-right, top-to-bottom order. Only one level of nesting is required.
problems.reference_solution:
def flatten(nested):
    return [x for inner in nested for x in inner]

variants.name: returns-input
variants.code:
def flatten(nested):
    return list(nested)

variants.name: first-row-only
variants.code:
def flatten(nested):
    return list(nested[0]) if nested else []

variants.name: swapped-order
variants.code:
def flatten(nested):
    return [x for inner in reversed(nested) for x in inner]

problems.title: Largest Sliding Window Sum
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums and a window size k, return the maximum sum of any contiguous sublist of length k. Assume k <= len(nums) and len(nums) >= 1.
problems.reference_solution:
def largest_window_sum(nums, k):
    return max(sum(nums[i:i + k]) for i in range(len(nums) - k + 1))

variants.name: returns-total-sum
variants.code:
def largest_window_sum(nums, k):
    return sum(nums)

variants.name: off-by-one-window
variants.code:
def largest_window_sum(nums, k):
    return max(sum(nums[i:i + k]) for i in range(len(nums) - k))

variants.name: min-not-max
variants.code:
def largest_window_sum(nums, k):
    return min(sum(nums[i:i + k]) for i in range(len(nums) - k + 1))

problems.title: Count Consonants
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return the number of consonant letters (alphabetic characters that are not in 'aeiou', case-insensitive).
problems.reference_solution:
def count_consonants(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c.isalpha() and c not in 'aeiou')

variants.name: returns-zero
variants.code:
def count_consonants(s):
    return 0

variants.name: counts-vowels-instead
variants.code:
def count_consonants(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c in 'aeiou')

variants.name: counts-all-chars
variants.code:
def count_consonants(s):
    return len(s)

problems.title: Capitalize Each Word
problems.problem_text: Given a string s of words separated by single spaces, return the string with each word's first letter uppercased and remaining letters lowercased. 'hELLO woRLD' becomes 'Hello World'.

problems.title: String to Integer (atoi-lite)
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return the integer it represents. Skip leading whitespace, support optional leading '+' or '-' sign, then read digit characters until a non-digit is found. Return 0 if no digits are found. Inputs fit in a regular Python int.
problems.reference_solution:
def atoi(s):
    s = s.lstrip()
    if not s:
        return 0
    sign = 1
    i = 0
    if s[0] in '+-':
        if s[0] == '-':
            sign = -1
        i = 1
    n = 0
    started = False
    while i < len(s) and s[i].isdigit():
        n = n * 10 + int(s[i])
        started = True
        i += 1
    return sign * n if started else 0

variants.name: no-sign-handling
ignores-whitespace-and-sign
returns-zero
variants.code:
def atoi(s):
    return 0

problems.title: Integer to Binary String
problems.problem_text: Given a non-negative integer n, return its binary representation as a string with no '0b' prefix. For 0 return '0'. For 5 return '101'.
problems.reference_solution:
def to_binary(n):
    return bin(n)[2:] if n > 0 else '0'

variants.name: leaves-prefix
variants.code:
def to_binary(n):
    return bin(n)

variants.name: returns-decimal-string
variants.code:
def to_binary(n):
    return str(n)

variants.name: off-by-one-base
variants.code:
def to_binary(n):
    return bin(n + 1)[2:] if n >= 0 else '0'

problems.title: Tower of Hanoi Move Count
problems.problem_text: Given a non-negative integer n (number of disks), return the minimum number of moves required to solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle: 2^n - 1. n=0 returns 0.
problems.reference_solution:
def hanoi_moves(n):
    return (1 << n) - 1

    return n

variants.code:

variants.name: linear-not-exponential

problems.title: Matrix Transpose
problems.problem_text: Given a non-empty rectangular matrix as a list of lists of integers, return the transposed matrix (rows become columns).
problems.reference_solution:
def transpose(m):
    return [list(row) for row in zip(*m)]

variants.name: returns-input
variants.code:
def transpose(m):
    return [list(row) for row in m]

variants.name: row-reverse-not-transpose
variants.code:
def transpose(m):
    return [list(reversed(row)) for row in m]

variants.name: transposes-then-reverses
variants.code:
def transpose(m):
    return [list(row)[::-1] for row in zip(*m)]
